claim: OpenAI Agent Builder is drag-and-drop UI for non-technical teams to build agents without coding skills
actionable: For non-technical internal users, use Agent Builder to democratize agent development without requiring AI engineer involvement
source_title: OpenAI DevDay 2025 - What Hit What Missed

claim: Agent Kit connector registry allows pre-built integrations with third-party services and MCP servers for tool composition
actionable: Use available connectors and MCP servers in Agent Builder before building custom integrations
source_title: OpenAI DevDay 2025 - What Hit What Missed

claim: GPT real-time mini is 70% cheaper than previous models, making real-time voice agents much more cost-effective
actionable: For voice agent projects, GPT real-time mini pricing now makes production voice apps economically viable
source_title: OpenAI DevDay 2025 - What Hit What Missed

claim: Sora 2 video API priced at 10 cents per second (basic) to 50 cents per second (pro), driving down video API costs
actionable: Sora 2 pricing will likely pressure competitors to reduce video generation costs; budget accordingly
source_title: OpenAI DevDay 2025 - What Hit What Missed

claim: Apps SDK for ChatGPT enables third-party apps but discovery remains challenging when competing against OpenAI partners
actionable: If building ChatGPT apps, differentiate through niche use cases rather than competing on general categories
